mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate transmits easier financial conditions to equities by lowering discount rates and improving risk appetite, so a sustained decline predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of months with a 3-month decline in FEDFUNDS, or if the rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE signals labor-market deterioration, weaker household income growth, and lower earnings expectations, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days after the rise exceeds a threshold.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the signal's hit rate is indistinguishable from random over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS growth and RSAFS both improve together, the economy is expanding with stronger payroll income and retail spending, which supports near-term corporate revenues and lifts S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition does not outperform either proxy alone in out-of-sample tests, or if the combined signal fails to produce positive excess 20-day returns in at least 55% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in INDPRO reflects industrial output contraction and weaker real activity, which should translate into lower corporate earnings expectations and negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward SPX returns are not negative on average during 3-month INDPRO declines, or if the signal's rolling 3-year IC remains above zero but insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation is high and FEDFUNDS remains far below it, real policy rates are deeply negative and policy is behind the curve, which should be bullish for equities over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation running above FEDFUNDS by > 2pp
failure_condition: Reject if the inflation-minus-policy-rate condition does not outperform a random benchmark, or if triggered periods fail to show positive 20-day SPX returns in at least 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A steep decline in HOUST indicates housing demand deterioration and weaker interest-sensitive spending, which should foreshadow lower S&P 500 returns over the next quarter as growth expectations are revised down.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward SPX returns are not below baseline after the housing-demand threshold is breached, or if the signal loses directional accuracy for two consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: A labor-market setup with falling PAYEMS and rising UNRATE signals income stress and deteriorating demand, and that combination should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if the two-series labor deterioration state does not outperform each single series alone, or if positive 20-day SPX returns occur in more than 45% of matched episodes.

mechanism_narrative: During an early-cycle rebound, rising GDPC1 and improving RSAFS indicate stronger real activity and sales momentum, which should generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint GDP-and-sales growth state fails to beat the unconditional 60-day return mean, or if the signal's rolling 2-year hit rate falls below 55%.
